title: Implement v2 Prospects page using legacy #customers section as reference
description: Build a new /dashboard/prospects route with a ProspectsTable and ProspectDetailModal, taking visual/UX cues from the legacy #customers section in website/dashboard.html but wiring it to /ai-crm/leads APIs.

steps.instruction: Create a new React route for /dashboard/prospects that renders a ProspectsTable component, taking the table structure and column naming inspiration from the #customers table in website/dashboard.html.

Ensure the ProspectsTable component uses the shared Prospect type from types/dashboard.ts (or create/extend that type if it does not yet exist). Include at least: id, name, score, status, source, lastContactedAt.

Wire the ProspectsTable to fetch data from a backend API, e.g. GET /ai-crm/leads, supporting basic query params for status, minimum score, and source. Implement client-side controls (dropdowns/inputs) for these filters, defaulting to sort by score descending.

Create a ProspectDetailModal component that displays full details for a selected prospect, including placeholders for scripts and recent activities so those can be wired up later. Take visual cues from any detail views or modals present in website/dashboard.html, but do not modify that file.

Integrate the ProspectDetailModal into the /dashboard/prospects page such that clicking a row in the ProspectsTable opens the modal and fetches detailed data from GET /ai-crm/leads/:id. Handle loading and error states gracefully.
Run linting and tests (or build) to ensure the new Prospects route and components are healthy.
commands: npm run lint || npm test || npm run build || echo 'no-check'
